summary: n8n-nodes-net-utils exfiltrates host recon and opens a reverse shell to 103.27.109.184 on require
details: The package advertises itself as an n8n community node but its main entrypoint (index.js) executes a hostile IIFE on require(). It runs `hostname`, `id`, `hostname -I`, `env`, `docker ps`, and `getent group docker`, then JSON-POSTs the output over raw TCP to the hardcoded host 103.27.109.184 on port 8890. It then spawns `/bin/bash -i` wired to a TCP socket at 103.27.109.184:8895 (via `/dev/tcp/`), giving the remote operator interactive shell access to the installer's machine. When Docker is available, it runs `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine` to read the host's /etc/shadow and hostname, and attempts to exec into a specific container to reach an internal admin endpoint, exfiltrating results to 103.27.109.184:8891. The declared n8n node (MyNode.js) is an empty stub — the n8n framing is cover for a load-time attack chain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
